svg.title: MN 9 Sammādiṭṭhi Sutta — Right View
svg.desc: A diagram of MN 9. Venerable Sāriputta gives sixteen expositions of right view: one discernment method — discerning what a phenomenon is, its arising, its cessation, and the way leading to its cessation — applied to the wholesome and unwholesome, the four nutriments, the four noble truths, the twelve links of dependent origination shown as a reverse chain from aging-and-death down to ignorance, and finally the taints. Every exposition closes with the same liberation refrain: abandoning the underlying tendencies, abandoning ignorance and arousing true knowledge, and making an end of suffering here and now.

g.text: “In what way is a disciple of the Noble Ones one of right view?”
straight view · unshakable clarity in the Dhamma · arrived at the true Dhamma
ujugatāssa diṭṭhi · dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgato · āgato imaṁ saddhammaṁ
“Right view, right view,” it is said — in what way is one of right view?
“Sammādiṭṭhi sammādiṭṭhī”ti vuccati · kittāvatā ariyasāvako sammādiṭṭhi hoti
Venerable Sāriputta to the bhikkhus at Jeta’s Grove · “Then listen, friends, and pay close attention.”
one method, applied to all sixteen — the shared “way” and liberation refrain are shown here once
When a noble disciple discerns X, its arising, its cessation, and the way leading to its cessation — in this way too they are one of right view.
yato kho · pajānāti · ettāvatāpi kho · sammādiṭṭhi hoti
LIBERATION REFRAIN · shared by all sixteen expositions
abandoned the underlying tendency to desire · dispelled the tendency to aversion
eradicated the tendency to the view and conceit “I am” · abandoned ignorance, aroused true knowledge
here and now makes an end of suffering
sabbaso rāgānusayaṁ pahāya · paṭighānusayaṁ paṭivinodetvā · “asmī”ti diṭṭhimānānusayaṁ samūhanitvā
avijjaṁ pahāya vijjaṁ uppādetvā · diṭṭheva dhamme dukkhassantakaro hoti
“Could there be another way?” · siyā panāvuso aññopi pariyāyo
1 · THE UNWHOLESOME AND THE WHOLESOME · AKUSALA / KUSALA
Discerning the unwholesome and the wholesome, and their roots, the noble disciple is one of right view.
yato kho · akusalañca pajānāti · kusalamūlañca pajānāti · sammādiṭṭhi hoti → + liberation refrain (see template)
Four nutriments for the maintenance of beings · cattāro āhārā bhūtānaṁ vā sattānaṁ ṭhitiyā
Arising: taṇhāsamudayā āhārasamudayo · Cessation: taṇhānirodhā āhāranirodho
3 · THE FOUR NOBLE TRUTHS · CATTĀRI ARIYASACCĀNI
in brief, the five aggregates of clinging
Birth is suffering, aging is suffering, illness is suffering, death is suffering; sorrow, lamentation, pain, displeasure, and despair are suffering;
association with the disliked is suffering; separation from the liked is suffering; not getting what one wishes for is suffering;
in brief, the five aggregates subject to clinging are suffering.
jātipi dukkhā · jarāpi dukkhā · byādhipi dukkho · maraṇampi dukkhaṁ … pañcupādānakkhandhā dukkhā
reverse chain — each link traces to its condition · every link resolves via the eightfold path + liberation refrain (see template)
broken teeth · gray hair · wrinkled skin;
passing away · breaking up of the aggregates
descent into the womb · manifestation of
the aggregates · obtaining the sense bases
three realms of existence — realm of sense desire (kāmabhavo) ·
four kinds: clinging to sensual pleasures · clinging to views ·
clinging to rules & observances · clinging to a doctrine of self
six classes: craving for forms · sounds · odors ·
flavors · tangibles · craving for mental objects
six classes: born of eye-contact, ear-contact, nose-contact,
eye · ear · nose · tongue · body · mind bases
name: Feeling, perception, intention, contact, and attention
form: four great elements + derived form
not knowing suffering, not knowing arising of suffering,
not knowing suffering’s cessation, not knowing the way to suffering’s cessation
Arising: avijjāsamudayā āsavasamudayo · Cessation: avijjānirodhā āsavanirodho
ignorance and the taints condition each other → eightfold path + liberation refrain (see template)
